commands.review_summary:
The PR tightens memory-core CLI numeric option parsing and memory-wiki `wiki apply --confidence` validation, with Commander-level regression tests.

PR surface: Source +38, Tests +70. Total +108 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection, though this read-only review did not run the CLI. Current main uses `Number(value)` for the affected options, so malformed inputs can become `NaN`, `Infinity`, or out-of-range numbers without Commander rejecting them.
commands.review_followup: No automated code repair is indicated on the latest head; maintainer review should focus on accepting the fail-fast CLI compatibility change and waiting for CI.

target.title: fix(memory): reject invalid CLI numeric options
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes memory CLI numeric option parsing so malformed values fail before command runtime.
- Replaces raw `Number(value)` parsers in `memory search` and `memory promote` options with a finite-number parser.
- Validates `wiki apply --confidence` against its documented 0..1 range before metadata mutation runs.
- Adds Commander-level regression coverage proving invalid numeric arguments stop before command actions.

## Linked context

Related: local clawpatch findings `fnd_sig-feat-cli-command-73043a9962-_3750639936`, `fnd_sig-feat-cli-command-609b283a4a-_ed967e8c44`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: memory CLI numeric options accepted `NaN`/`Infinity`; wiki apply confidence accepted non-finite and out-of-range values.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, actual `pnpm openclaw` CLI invocations, focused Vitest via repo wrapper.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm openclaw memory search hello --max-results nope --json`; `pnpm openclaw wiki apply synthesis CLIProof --body proof --source-id source.alpha --confidence 1.5 --json`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/cli.test.ts extensions/memory-wiki/src/cli.test.ts`.
Evidence after fix: memory command exited 1 with `option '--max-results <n>' argument 'nope' is invalid. --max-results must be a finite number.`; wiki command exited 1 with `option '--confidence <n>' argument '1.5' is invalid. --confidence must be a number between 0 and 1.`; focused Vitest passed 2 files / 68 tests.
Observed result after fix: invalid memory numeric options and invalid wiki confidence values fail before manager lookup or wiki mutation actions run.
What was not tested: full repository check suite.
Proof limitations or environment constraints: proof is focused to the changed plugin CLI parser surfaces.
Before evidence: code inspection showed affected options used raw `Number(value)`, which can produce `NaN` or `Infinity` without parser rejection.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/cli.test.ts extensions/memory-wiki/src/cli.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/memory-core/src/cli.ts extensions/memory-core/src/cli.test.ts extensions/memory-wiki/src/cli.ts extensions/memory-wiki/src/cli.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` -> `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`, invalid CLI numeric values now fail fast with explicit parser errors.
Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`.
Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`.
What is the highest-risk area? Scripts that accidentally passed malformed memory CLI numeric options will now fail instead of continuing with `NaN` or `Infinity`.
How is that risk mitigated? Valid finite values keep existing semantics, and the wiki confidence parser enforces the command's documented 0..1 range.

## Current review state

What is the next action? Review and CI.
What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? GitHub CI on the pushed branch.
Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? Autoreview findings addressed; final branch autoreview clean.
